revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Stretched_burial]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
体全体を伸ばした状態で埋葬すること、またそのような埋葬の方法。基本的には、[[屈葬]]と区別する用語であるので、腕をのばしている場合、胸の上に手を置く場合、仰向けの場合（仰臥伸展葬）、うつ伏せの場合が考えられる。研究者間では、単純に遺体が埋葬された場合の姿勢だけではなく、遺体を伸展葬しうる容器としての棺、伸展葬を意識している包装の出現があってはじめて、屈葬と区別する意義があると考えられている。

[[文化人類学]]、[[考古学]]において、体全体を伸ばした状態で埋葬すること、またそのような埋葬の方法のことを指す。基本的には、[[屈葬]]と区別する用語であるので、腕をのばしている場合、胸の上に手を置く場合、仰向けの場合（仰臥伸展葬）、うつ伏せの場合が考えられる。研究者間では、単純に遺体が埋葬された場合の姿勢だけではなく、遺体を伸展葬しうる容器としての棺、伸展葬を意識している包装の出現があってはじめて、屈葬と区別する意義があると考えられている。

[[文化人類学]]、[[考古学]]において、体全体を伸ばした状態で埋葬すること、またそのような埋葬の方法のことを指す。基本的には、[[屈葬]]と区別する用語であるので、腕をのばしている場合、胸の上に手を置く場合、仰向けの場合（仰臥伸展葬）、うつ伏せ（俯臥）の場合が考えられる。研究者間では、単純に遺体が埋葬された場合の姿勢だけではなく、遺体を伸展葬しうる容器としての棺、伸展葬を意識している包装の出現があってはじめて、屈葬と区別する意義があると考えられている。

revision.comment: Created page with "伸展葬"
revision.text: 伸展葬

[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[cultural anthropology]] and [[archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from [[kusosō]] (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
{{Funerary practices}}
[[Category:Burial customs in archaeological contexts]] [[Category:Burials]] [[Category:Posture]] [[ja:伸展葬]]
伸展葬

伸展葬

[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[cultural anthropology]] and [[archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
{{Funerary practices}}
[[Category:Burial customs in archaeological contexts]] [[Category:Burials]] [[Category:Posture]] [[ja:伸展葬]]
伸展葬

伸展葬

[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[cultural anthropology]] and [[archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
{{Funerary practices}}

[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[cultural anthropology]] and [[archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
{{Funerary practices}}

revision.text:
[[画像:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|北米アデナ文化の墓に見られる仰臥伸展葬]] '''伸展葬'''（しんてんそう、extended burial）とは、[[文化人類学]]や[[考古学]]において体全体を伸ばした状態ですること、またそのような[[埋葬]]の方法のことを指す。基本的には、[[屈葬]]と区別する用語であるので、腕をのばしている場合、胸の上に手を置く場合、仰向けの場合（仰臥伸展葬）、うつ伏せ（俯臥）の場合が考えられる。研究者間では、単純に遺体が埋葬された場合の姿勢だけではなく、遺体を伸展葬しうる容器としての棺、伸展葬を意識している包装の出現があってはじめて、屈葬と区別する意義があると考えられている。
==参考文献== * 水野清一、小林行雄編『図解考古学辞典』,創元新社,1959年
{{葬制}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しんてんそう}} [[category:考古遺跡に見る葬制]] [[Category:埋葬]] [[category:姿勢]]

[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]]
'''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[:en:Cultural anthropology|Cultural anthropology]] and [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial). 

Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.

== References ==
* Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11381557]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]]
'''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[:en:Cultural anthropology|Cultural anthropology]] and [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial). 

Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.

== References ==
* Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.

{{Funerary practices}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]] {{Expand Japanese|伸展葬|date=November 2025}} <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381557]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in [[:en:Cultural anthropology|Cultural anthropology]] and [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
{{Funerary practices}}
伸展葬

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伸展葬]]
[[File:Adena burial in a simple log tomb.JPG|frame|right|An extended burial in a tomb of the Adena culture, North America.]] '''Shintensō''' (伸展葬, extended burial) refers to a burial method in {{ill|Cultural anthropology|en|Cultural anthropology|WD=Q28598|lt=Cultural anthropology|1=Cultural anthropology}} and {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} where the body is placed in a fully extended position. This term primarily distinguishes it from {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|lt=kusosō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} (flexed burial). Variations include arms placed at the sides, hands positioned on the chest, or different orientations such as lying face-up (supine extended burial) or face-down (prone burial).
Among researchers, the distinction between shintensō and kusosō is not based solely on the posture of the body during burial. It is also defined by the presence of burial containers, such as coffins, or wrappings that explicitly support extended burial practices.
== References == * Mizuno Seiichi and Kobayashi Yukio, eds. *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Sōgensha, 1959.
